refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-003 Refined: The NGHE solution shall enable operators and tele-operators to achieve a measured energy efficiency of energy_per_ton ≤ E_target (customer-defined threshold) over the agreed evaluation period, through integrated monitoring, control optimization, and feedback mechanisms.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is outcome-focused but lacks clarity on the mechanisms by which efficiency is achieved. Refinement introduces the key actors (operators, tele-operators), the measurement basis (energy_per_ton metric), and the temporal scope (evaluation period). This makes the requirement testable and traceable to system capabilities.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure total energy consumed (kWh) and total tonnage moved during the evaluation period; compute energy_per_ton; compare against E_target. Verification requires instrumentation of power consumption and load tracking across Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-003.1: The Vehicle shall measure and report real-time energy consumption (kW) to the NGHE Solution.
CR-NGHE-003.2: The Vehicle shall measure and report real-time load moved (tons) to the NGHE Solution.
CR-NGHE-003.3: The NGHE Solution shall aggregate energy and load data and compute energy_per_ton KPI.
CR-NGHE-003.4: The NGHE Solution shall provide energy efficiency feedback to operators and tele-operators to enable informed operational decisions.
CR-NGHE-003.5: The NGHE Solution shall log energy and load metrics for post-evaluation analysis and reporting.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement a centralized energy management system within the NGHE Solution that collects telemetry from Vehicle (power, load), computes energy_per_ton in real-time, and broadcasts efficiency feedback via Telemetry interface to operator displays.

DP-B: Distribute energy efficiency monitoring across Vehicle (local power/load measurement) and NGHE Solution (aggregation and KPI computation), with feedback routed through Teleoperation subsystem for tele-operator visibility.

DP-C: Implement energy optimization at the Vehicle control level (e.g., transmission efficiency, load balancing) coupled with NGHE Solution-level monitoring and feedback, creating a closed-loop efficiency control system.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-B: Distribute energy efficiency monitoring across Vehicle (local power/load measurement and reporting via Telemetry) and NGHE Solution (aggregation, KPI computation, and feedback distribution via Telemetry and Tasking interfaces). This approach leverages existing Vehicle instrumentation, minimizes coupling between Vehicle and NGHE Solution logic, and enables both direct operator feedback (via Vehicle displays) and tele-operator feedback (via Teleoperation subsystem).

potential_sources: Vehicle telemetry schema must be standardized to ensure NGHE Solution can reliably parse power and load data.
Feedback latency: real-time KPI computation and display refresh must meet operator perception thresholds (typically <500ms for control feedback).
Data consistency: energy and load measurements must be synchronized in time to avoid spurious KPI spikes.
coupling_check.notes: DP-B avoids tight coupling by using well-defined Telemetry and Tasking interfaces as the contract between Vehicle and NGHE Solution. Coupling risk is moderate and manageable through interface specification and testing.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Vehicle is equipped with power and load sensors; if not, retrofit cost and schedule impact must be assessed.
Assumption: Telemetry interface has sufficient bandwidth and latency characteristics to support real-time KPI feedback.
Risk: If Vehicle sensors are unreliable or drift over time, KPI measurements will be inaccurate, undermining the efficiency target.
Risk: Operator behavior and worksite conditions (terrain, load density) are primary drivers of energy_per_ton; system design alone cannot guarantee target achievement without operational discipline.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the E_target value and evaluation period duration with the customer before detailed design. Specify sensor accuracy requirements (e.g., ±5% for power, ±3% for load) and telemetry update frequency (e.g., 1 Hz minimum) to ensure KPI reliability.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module owns the energy efficiency KPI computation, aggregation, and feedback logic. It receives telemetry from Vehicle (power, load) via the Telemetry interface, computes energy_per_ton, and distributes feedback to operators and tele-operators via Telemetry (for direct operator displays) and Teleoperation (for tele-operator consoles). The module also logs KPI data for post-evaluation analysis.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Inbound: Vehicle power consumption (kW) and load moved (tons) at 1 Hz or higher. Outbound: Computed energy_per_ton KPI, efficiency status (e.g., on-target, above-target), and advisory feedback to operator displays.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Inbound: Evaluation period start/stop signals and E_target threshold updates from Site Manager or System Engineer.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Outbound: If energy consumption anomalies are detected (e.g., runaway power draw indicating mechanical failure), alert Safety Services to prevent unsafe operation.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural owner of system-level KPI computation and feedback logic. Energy efficiency is a system-level concern that requires aggregation across Vehicle subsystems. Containment is high; minimal diffusion risk.

Vehicle could own local power/load measurement and reporting (which it does), but KPI computation and feedback logic belong at the system level. If KPI logic were embedded in Vehicle, it would be duplicated across multiple vehicle types and tele-operation modes, creating high diffusion and maintenance burden.

Teleoperation subsystem is responsible for remote operator interface and control, not energy efficiency KPI computation. Placing KPI logic here would couple efficiency monitoring to tele-operation mode only, excluding direct operators. High diffusion risk.

Safety Services owns safety-critical alerts and interlocks. Energy efficiency KPI is a performance metric, not a safety function. Placing it here would blur the boundary between safety and performance, creating confusion and maintenance risk.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the correct owner of CR-NGHE-003 because: (1) energy efficiency is a system-level KPI that requires aggregation of Vehicle telemetry; (2) feedback must be distributed to both direct operators and tele-operators, requiring a central coordination point; (3) post-evaluation analysis and reporting are system-level concerns; (4) containment in NGHE Solution avoids duplication and diffusion across Vehicle variants and operational modes.
containment_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution becomes a monolithic module, KPI computation logic may be difficult to test and maintain. Mitigation: Decompose KPI computation into a dedicated sub-module (e.g., EnergyEfficiencyMonitor) within NGHE Solution.

Risk: Telemetry interface must be reliable and low-latency; if Vehicle telemetry is delayed or lossy, KPI feedback will be stale or inaccurate. Mitigation: Specify telemetry SLA (e.g., 99.5% availability, <100ms latency) and implement buffering/interpolation logic in NGHE Solution.

Risk: E_target may vary by customer or worksite; if NGHE Solution hard-codes the threshold, it will lack flexibility. Mitigation: Make E_target a configurable parameter, updated via Tasking interface.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define the internal structure of NGHE Solution to separate concerns: (1) Telemetry Aggregator: collects and buffers Vehicle telemetry; (2) KPI Engine: computes energy_per_ton and efficiency status; (3) Feedback Distributor: routes feedback to operator displays and tele-operator consoles; (4) Data Logger: persists KPI data for analysis. This decomposition will improve testability and maintainability.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-B (distributed monitoring and centralized KPI computation) directly addresses the refined FR. The DP specifies how Vehicle telemetry is collected, aggregated, and fed back to operators and tele-operators to enable achievement of the energy_per_ton target. The mapping is clear and traceable.

diffusion_risks: Telemetry interface must be standardized across all Vehicle types to ensure NGHE Solution can reliably parse power and load data. If Vehicle telemetry schemas diverge, NGHE Solution logic will become fragmented.
Feedback latency: if Telemetry interface has high latency (>500ms), operator feedback will be stale, reducing effectiveness. Mitigation: specify telemetry SLA.

Data consistency: power and load measurements must be time-synchronized. If Vehicle reports them asynchronously, KPI spikes may occur. Mitigation: implement buffering and interpolation in NGHE Solution.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the correct owner of KPI computation and feedback logic. Vehicle owns telemetry generation; Teleoperation owns tele-operator interface. Interfaces are well-defined (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert). Containment is high; diffusion risk is low if interfaces are properly specified and enforced.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible: measure total energy and load during evaluation period, compute energy_per_ton, compare against E_target. Requires instrumentation of Vehicle power/load sensors and NGHE Solution KPI computation. Test plan should include nominal operation, sensor failure modes, and edge cases (e.g., zero load, high power transients).
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: Vehicle measures and reports power/load; NGHE Solution aggregates and computes KPI; Teleoperation and operator displays present feedback. Ownership boundaries are well-defined. Interfaces are explicit (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert).

recommended_revisions: Specify telemetry interface schema (power units, load units, update frequency, timestamp format) to ensure Vehicle and NGHE Solution interoperability.
Define E_target as a configurable parameter, updated via Tasking interface, to support customer-specific and worksite-specific targets.
Specify feedback latency SLA (e.g., <500ms from measurement to operator display) to ensure operator perception and control effectiveness.
Define sensor accuracy requirements (e.g., ±5% for power, ±3% for load) to ensure KPI reliability.
Specify post-evaluation analysis and reporting format (e.g., CSV, JSON) to support customer review and compliance verification.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-003 is not atomic; it comprises multiple sub-functions (telemetry collection, KPI computation, feedback distribution, data logging) that require further decomposition. The refined FR and selected DP are sound, and the IM assignment to NGHE Solution is well-justified. However, to ensure detailed design quality and traceability, each sub-FR must be decomposed to the next level, with explicit DP and IM assignments. This will clarify internal module boundaries, interface contracts, and ownership within NGHE Solution.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-003.1: The Vehicle shall measure and report real-time energy consumption (kW) to the NGHE Solution.
CR-NGHE-003.2: The Vehicle shall measure and report real-time load moved (tons) to the NGHE Solution.
CR-NGHE-003.3: The NGHE Solution shall aggregate energy and load data and compute energy_per_ton KPI.
CR-NGHE-003.4: The NGHE Solution shall provide energy efficiency feedback to operators and tele-operators to enable informed operational decisions.
CR-NGHE-003.5: The NGHE Solution shall log energy and load metrics for post-evaluation analysis and reporting.

assumptions: Vehicle is equipped with power and load sensors with ±5% and ±3% accuracy, respectively.
Telemetry interface supports 1 Hz or higher update frequency with <100ms latency.
E_target is customer-defined and may vary by worksite; it will be provided via Tasking interface.
Operator behavior and worksite conditions are primary drivers of energy_per_ton; system design enables but does not guarantee target achievement.
Post-evaluation analysis is performed offline; real-time KPI feedback is the primary mechanism for operator guidance.
open_risks: Sensor reliability: if Vehicle power/load sensors drift or fail, KPI measurements will be inaccurate. Mitigation: implement sensor health monitoring and anomaly detection in NGHE Solution.
Telemetry latency: if Telemetry interface has high latency, operator feedback will be stale. Mitigation: specify and enforce telemetry SLA.
Data consistency: if power and load measurements are not time-synchronized, KPI spikes may occur. Mitigation: implement buffering and interpolation in NGHE Solution.
Operator adoption: if operators do not understand or trust the KPI feedback, they may ignore it. Mitigation: design intuitive feedback UI and conduct operator training.

Safety criticality: energy efficiency is marked as high safety criticality. If efficiency monitoring interferes with safety-critical functions (e.g., emergency stop), it could create hazards. Mitigation: ensure SafetyAlert interface is properly integrated and tested.
evidence_needed: Vehicle sensor specifications (power, load, accuracy, update frequency).
Telemetry interface specification (schema, latency, bandwidth, reliability).
Customer-defined E_target value and evaluation period duration.
Operator feedback UI mockups and usability testing results.
Safety analysis (FMEA, HARA) to ensure energy efficiency monitoring does not create safety hazards.
Test plan for KPI computation, including nominal operation, sensor failure modes, and edge cases.
Post-evaluation analysis and reporting format specification.
